summary: No role-based access control (RBAC) implemented. Admin routes have zero authorization checks.

tests.evidence: No Depends(verify_admin) or equivalent in route signature
tests.notes: Any user — not just admins — can read all scan logs with pagination controls

tests.evidence: No authorization dependency in get_stats handler
tests.notes: Aggregate statistics about threat volumes and categories are freely exposed

tests.evidence: No authorization dependency in export_csv handler. Returns StreamingResponse of all rows.
tests.notes: Complete database export (all scan logs including user-submitted input_data) is accessible to everyone. This is the highest-impact finding.

tests.actual_access: ALLOW — also accepts arbitrary filter manipulation
tests.category: Insecure Direct Object Access / Filter Injection
tests.evidence: Query params `feature` and `verdict` are directly passed to SQLAlchemy filter with no allowlist validation (lines 24-27 admin.py)
tests.notes: While SQLAlchemy ORM prevents raw SQL injection, there is no validation that `feature` or `verdict` values are from a known-good set. Sending feature='; DROP TABLE scan_logs;--' will be safely rejected by ORM but unexpected values may cause info leakage in error responses.

tests.actual_access: ALLOW — per_page parameter allows large data dumps
tests.category: Insecure Pagination / DoS via Mass Data Fetch
tests.evidence: per_page: int = Query(20, ge=1, le=100) — max 100 rows per request. While bounded, combined with zero auth this enables full database enumeration with ~total/100 requests
tests.notes: An unauthenticated attacker can iterate pages to dump the entire scan_logs table
